source: Generating Better Search Engine Text Advertisements with Deep Reinforcement Learning.pdf

concepts.finding:

CONCLUSION
We applied Self Critical Sequence Training to the problem of gener-
ating attractive search engine advertisements, using a novel method
through which we optimized a non-differentiable metric not related
to similarity between output and training data.
concepts.implication: Relevant for similarity-based retrieval and lookalike targeting

concepts.finding:
We frame the generation of search engine
text ads as a sequence to sequence problem, and consider two re-
lated goals: to generate ads similar to those a human would write,
and to generate ads with high click-through rates.
concepts.implication: Relevant for similarity-based retrieval and lookalike targeting

concepts.finding:
3.6
Relationship to Previous Self Critical
Sequence Training Approaches
Our approach is algebraically similar to those proposed by previous
SCST literature ([17, 28]), but differs fundamentally because of the
reward function used.
concepts.implication: Relevant for similarity-based retrieval and lookalike targeting

concepts.finding:
For
simplicity, we set the user query equal to a keyword corresponding
to each landing page submitted by the advertiser, and assumed that
all advertisements appear in the same location (as the first “search
result,” or “mainline-1” in advertising terminology).
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
KEYWORDS
abstractive summarization, deep reinforcement learning, seq2seq,
click prediction, sponsored search, ad creative optimization
ACM Reference Format:
J Weston Hughes, Keng-hao Chang, and Ruofei Zhang.
concepts.implication: Directly applicable to CTR/CVR prediction in advertising systems

concepts.finding:
When a user submits
a query, advertisements relevant to that query (as determined by
a ranking algorithm and hand- or machine-specified keywords)
are displayed along with search results.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
We propose a novel application of Self Critical Se-
quence Training using a reward function not tied to the training
data to maximize some desiderata (described in sections 3.5 and
3.6).
concepts.implication: Can optimize long-term user engagement metrics

concepts.finding:
In the search engine context, however,
pre-written promotional text is not consistently available unless
submitted by advertisers, defeating the purpose of generating the
ad.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization
concepts.open_question:
How to address the limitation described: In the search engine context, however,
pre-written promotional text is not consistently available unless
submitted by advertisers, defeating the purpose of generating the
ad.

concepts.finding:
In
[26], the relevance step is performed by finding n-grams that occur
frequently together in a landing page, with the expectation that the
most frequent n-grams will be the most important.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
Our method does the opposite: training towards sampled
points with higher predicted CTR may pull the model away from
the training corpus and cause it to predict novel advertisements
with a higher click rate than those written by humans.
concepts.implication: Directly applicable to CTR/CVR prediction in advertising systems

concepts.finding:
After training the first set of models we noted that many of
the generated ads contained repeated words and phrases despite
the use of intra-temporal attention (this is a common problem in
sequence to sequence settings [2]).
concepts.implication: Architecture pattern applicable to sequence modeling for user behavior

concepts.finding:
6
DISCUSSION
The major contribution of this work is the novel application of
Self-Critical Sequence Training to maximize a non-differentiable
reward unrelated to the match between the training and predicted
data points.
concepts.implication: Can optimize long-term user engagement metrics

concepts.finding:
They also achieve
a higher predicted click rate, suggesting that the model is optimiz-
ing click rate within the distribution of human-like ads it learns
from the training set, finding a part of the distribution with the
highest expected click rate.
concepts.implication: Directly applicable to CTR/CVR prediction in advertising systems

concepts.finding:
We demonstrate that
this method improves the expected click rate and human-rated at-
tractiveness of advertisements generated by a sequence to sequence
model.
concepts.implication: Directly applicable to CTR/CVR prediction in advertising systems

ABSTRACT
Deep Reinforcement Lear
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
We jointly train a
model to minimize cross-entropy on an existing corpus of Landing
Page/Text Ad pairs using typical sequence to sequence training
techniques while also optimizing the expected click-through rate
(CTR) as predicted by an existing oracle model using SCST.
concepts.implication: Directly applicable to CTR/CVR prediction in advertising systems

INTRODUCTION
Search engines derive a large portion of their revenue from showing
text advertisements along with search results.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
Advertisers
typically only pay when their ad is clicked on by a user, so it ben-
efits both search engines and advertisers when ads are attractive
and relevant [25].
concepts.implication: Directly applicable to CTR/CVR prediction in advertising systems
